channel.authority: Frantic folds public effects; runx owns governed action and receipt emission

bounty.title: Run a public runx registry install smoke matrix
bounty.description:
Run a public runx registry install smoke matrix

Review criteria before you claim.
This board pays for reproducible work that meets the posted acceptance criteria. Every delivery is verified and its evidence is checked before payout.
- Dogfood the work. Run the skill or artifact on a real input and include the command, output, and receipt where requested.
- Make the proof checkable. Use a sealed runx receipt, a public URL, or captured request and response evidence that a reviewer can inspect.
- Keep claims tied to sources. Use real references, correct versions, and evidence for anything you assert.
- Ship something with public or operator value. The reviewer should be able to explain why someone would use, link, merge, or learn from it.
- Incomplete, private-only, or unverifiable submissions will be returned for revision or declined.

Context. Published runx skills only matter if a new user can find, install, inspect, run, and verify them. Dogfood at least two public registry packages from a clean environment and publish the transcript. Blocking install or verification failures should become public runx issues.

Deliverable: A public runx registry install smoke-matrix report with public_url, evidence_json, receipt_ref, and report artifacts.

Acceptance:
- The governed receipt or validation run uses runx CLI 0.6.6 or newer; evidence_json.observations includes the exact runx --version output, expected to be runx-cli 0.6.6 or newer.
- The report names OS, shell, install method, fresh runx home path or isolation method, and exact runx --version output.
- The report covers at least two public runx registry packages.
- For each package, the report captures registry read and install command output.
- For each package, the report runs or inspects the harness or published usage path.
- The report records receipt, verify result, or exact failure for each package.
- Blocking install, registry, or verify failures get linked public runx issues unless already tracked.
- evidence_json observations include environment, package refs, registry read, install, run/harness, receipt or failure, verify result, and follow-up links.

criteria.antiFake: Screenshots alone, private dashboards, invented API responses, one-line opinions, and findings without captured request or response evidence are not sufficient for review.
acceptance: The governed receipt or validation run uses runx CLI 0.6.6 or newer; evidence_json.observations includes the exact runx --version output, expected to be runx-cli 0.6.6 or newer.
The report names OS, shell, install method, fresh runx home path or isolation method, and exact runx --version output.
The report covers at least two public runx registry packages.
For each package, the report captures registry read and install command output.
For each package, the report runs or inspects the harness or published usage path.
The report records receipt, verify result, or exact failure for each package.
Blocking install, registry, or verify failures get linked public runx issues unless already tracked.
evidence_json observations include environment, package refs, registry read, install, run/harness, receipt or failure, verify result, and follow-up links.
criteria.reviewGate: Re-run two captured checks from evidence_json and confirm the report explains impact, reproduction, exact source of truth, and why a real operator or maintainer should act on it.
criteria.deliverable: A public runx registry install smoke-matrix report with public_url, evidence_json, receipt_ref, and report artifacts.

events.text: #47 · Run a public runx registry install smoke matrix

AUTO REVIEW #47: blocked before human review (weak 2/5) · The work shows signs of real execution: specific package digests, ed25519 trust labels, harness failure reasons, and version-pinned CLI. But the delivery misses the contract's core form requirement. What is missing an...

#47 · Not accepted. The smoke matrix asserts outcomes like Success and Installed without the captured registry-read and install command output the bounty requires, the runx version is stated rather than captured, and the receipt is a birth receipt rather than a governed run. Re-run with the actual command transcripts pasted per package and a real run receipt. · quality 2/5 weak

AUTO REVIEW #47: ready for human review (strong 4/5) · The delivery passes all core acceptance bullets. Two public packages are covered (runx/dependency-cve-audit@sha-e11c90bbeb16 and ryde-play/dep-upgrade-plan@sha-7a1f34b0). Registry read, install, and inspect outputs ar...

#47 · Rejected, 1/5. Falsified: the CVE package run actually failed (exit 1, MODULE_NOT_FOUND) but the matrix reports all-green, and local-dev keys are labeled production. The one real finding (the registry package ships broken) is buried under a fake pass. Misrepresenting a failed run as green is a hard reject. · quality 1/5 poor
